target.name: Physical Intelligence
target.founder: Karol Hausman, Sergey Levine, Chelsea Finn, Brian Ichter

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $5.6B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $500M - $32.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Safe Superintelligence
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $32B

private.name: World Labs
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $5B+

private.name: Nominal
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $1B

private.name: Photoroom
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
Physical Intelligence is a Series B AI & Software company seeking valuation assessment. The target raised $600M+ against a current post-money valuation of $5.6B. The applicable comp universe consists of 9 private companies and 1 public comparable (Palantir, ticker PLTR), with primary peers including Safe Superintelligence ($32.0B Series B), World Labs ($5.0B Series B), Nominal ($1.0B Series B), and Photoroom ($500M Series B).

Two triangulation methods apply. Stage-Progression analysis uses the typical 1.9x multiplier for Series B advancement, yielding $10.6B. Peer-Median analysis across the 4 same-stage, same-sector comparables produces a median valuation of $3.0B, though the peer set exhibits wide dispersion (range: $500M to $32.0B), reflecting heterogeneity within frontier AI. The median peer is substantially below the target's last valuation.

The triangulated analysis supports a range of $2.5B (low) to $12.2B (high), with a median of $6.8B. The current $5.6B valuation sits near the center of this range, slightly below the median estimate. Key uncertainties include the wide dispersion among Series B AI peers—a 64x spread from Photoroom to Safe Superintelligence—and the founder team's ability to demonstrate differentiated technical or commercial progress. The peer-median method's relevance is limited by the heterogeneous risk profiles and market positioning within the sample.
